title: Galarhoeus buhsei
intro: Galarhoeus buhsei, also known as Buhse's creeping thyme, is a species of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Galarhoeus buhsei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
uses: Galarhoeus buhsei is traditionally used in Thai medicine. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health issues. Its leaves are also used to manage fever and relieve pain. Additionally, Galarhoeus buhsei is thought to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of the plant.
